enrichment.short_description: Sched is an event management platform for registration, scheduling, speaker coordination, and attendee check-in at conferences.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sched: event scheduling, registration, speaker management, and a mobile event app for conferences and PD events.

features.title: Event scheduling & session planning
features.description: Build multi-track schedules with room/venue assignments and personalized attendee itineraries.

features.title: Registration & ticketing
features.description: Sell tickets and manage attendee registration directly within the platform.

features.title: Event app
features.description: A web and mobile event app gives attendees access to schedules, speaker profiles, and networking tools.

features.title: Call for Papers
features.description: Collect and manage speaker and session submissions, with an advanced version available on higher tiers.

features.title: Speaker management
features.description: Coordinate speaker profiles, communications, and session assignments.

features.title: Attendee check-in & lead retrieval
features.description: Track attendance, print customizable badges, and capture leads on-site.

features.title: AI event planner
features.description: An AI-assisted planning tool is offered to help set up events.

features.title: Compliance & security
features.description: SOC 2 Type II certified, and GDPR, CCPA, and FERPA compliant.

pros: SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR/CCPA/FERPA compliant, relevant for schools and healthcare training organizations
30-day free trial plus a 60-day money-back guarantee
Unlimited free events for gatherings under 50 attendees (100 on the Ultra plan)
Used by institutions including Yale, Princeton, Stanford, and Harvard
Combines registration, scheduling, speaker management, and an event app in one platform
cons: Paid plan pricing isn't published; tiers require a 250+ attendee minimum and a sales quote
Advanced features like enhanced Call for Papers and lead retrieval are locked to higher tiers
Useful add-on bundles (support, migration, insights) are sold separately at extra cost

faqs.question: Does Sched have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes — events with fewer than 50 attendees (100 on the Ultra plan) can run for free.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Sched offers a 30-day free trial, with longer trials available on request, plus a 60-day money-back guarantee.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Sched hold?
faqs.answer: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA, FERPA, and ISO certification.

faqs.question: Does Sched offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sched includes an AI event planner tool.

faqs.question: Who uses Sched?
faqs.answer: Sched lists customers including Yale, Princeton, Stanford, Harvard, Chicago Public Schools, and the YMCA.

use_cases.title: Professional development tracking for schools
use_cases.description: K-12 and higher-ed institutions use Sched to schedule PD sessions and track PD hours for compliance reporting.

use_cases.title: Multi-track conferences
use_cases.description: Organizers build multi-room, multi-track schedules with speaker profiles and a Call for Papers workflow.

use_cases.title: Corporate learning events
use_cases.description: Corporate L&D teams use Sched's registration, badge printing, and lead retrieval tools for internal training events.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sched?
paragraphs: Sched is an event management platform that handles registration, scheduling, speaker coordination, and attendee management for conferences, conventions, professional-development events, and festivals. It has been used to run events since 2008.

The platform combines a web and mobile event app, a Call for Papers tool for collecting speaker submissions, on-site check-in and badge printing, and lead retrieval, alongside SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA, and FERPA compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sched is for
paragraphs: Sched is aimed at K-12 schools, higher-education institutions, nonprofits, corporate L&D departments, and healthcare and medical training organizations that need to schedule sessions, register attendees, and track professional-development hours. Sched lists customers including Yale, Princeton, Stanford, Harvard, and Chicago Public Schools.

enrichment.short_description: Sessionize is a cloud platform for managing conference Call for Papers, speaker submissions, and schedule building.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sessionize: Call for Papers, speaker management, and schedule-building software used by 31,000+ event organizers.

features.title: Call for Papers
features.description: Collect and evaluate session submissions with customizable evaluation workflows.

features.title: Speaker management
features.description: Communicate with speakers and manage public speaker profiles.

features.title: Drag-and-drop schedule builder
features.description: Build and publish event schedules visually.

features.title: Mobile & web apps
features.description: Attendees can browse sessions and schedules via mobile and web apps.

features.title: Unlimited team members
features.description: Add unlimited collaborators to help manage an event.

features.title: Task lists & embedding
features.description: Track organizing tasks and embed schedules or CFP forms on external sites.

features.title: JSON/XML export
features.description: Export session and schedule data for use in other systems.

pros: Free tier available for qualifying community events
Test mode available with no credit card required before paying
Large existing user base: 31,000+ event organizers, 298,000+ speakers, 11,600+ events, 1.1M+ session submissions
All features included on every paid plan (no feature gating)
Flat one-time event fee rather than a recurring subscription
cons: Free plan is restricted to community events and excluded for private, promotional, or commercial events
Professional Event pricing is a flat $499 regardless of event size
No AI features mentioned
Bulk/multi-event pricing requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Is Sessionize free?
faqs.answer: Yes, for qualifying free community events; commercial or private events require the paid Professional Event plan.

faqs.question: How much does Sessionize cost for a single event?
faqs.answer: The Professional Event plan is $499 USD + tax per event occurrence.

faqs.question: Can I try Sessionize before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fully functional test mode is available without a credit card.

faqs.question: Does Sessionize offer volume pricing?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Bulk plan offers custom pricing for organizers running 5+ events per year.

faqs.question: How many organizers use Sessionize?
faqs.answer: Sessionize reports over 31,000 event organizers and 298,000 speakers on the platform.

use_cases.title: Conference Call for Papers
use_cases.description: Organizers open a CFP, collect and evaluate submissions, then convert accepted talks into a published schedule.

use_cases.title: Community meetups & user groups
use_cases.description: Free community events can run their CFP and speaker management at no cost.

use_cases.title: Agencies running multiple events
use_cases.description: Agencies purchase Bulk activation codes to manage CFPs across 5+ events per year.

features: Test mode with no credit card required
Best value for agencies and corporations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sessionize?
paragraphs: Sessionize is a cloud-based platform, live since 2016, for managing a conference's Call for Papers, speaker submissions, and schedule building. Organizers use it to collect talk proposals, evaluate them, communicate with speakers, and publish a drag-and-drop schedule.
The platform reports over 31,000 event organizers, 298,000 speakers, and more than 1.1 million session submissions processed through its Call for Papers tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sessionize is for
paragraphs: Sessionize is built for conference and event organizers who need to run a Call for Papers, from free community meetups and user groups to paid single conferences and agencies coordinating multiple events per year.

enrichment.short_description: Pretalx is open-source conference management software for Call for Papers, speaker review, and schedule building.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pretalx: open-source Call for Papers and conference schedule software, free to test and self-hostable, used by PyCon and MozFest.

features.title: Call for Papers
features.description: Customizable CFP forms and submission management.

features.title: Anonymous or open review
features.description: Choose anonymous or open review processes for talk selection.

features.title: Speaker communication
features.description: Email speakers directly from the platform, with proof-reading support.

features.title: Schedule building
features.description: Build multi-track, multi-room schedules with automated change publication.

features.title: Self-hosting or managed hosting
features.description: Run pretalx on your own infrastructure or use the managed hosting at pretalx.com.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Extend functionality through community-built plugins.

features.title: Open source
features.description: Source code is publicly available with an open contributor ecosystem.

pros: Free to test with no hidden costs; you only pay once you make an event public on managed hosting
Open source, with publicly available source code and a plugin ecosystem
Self-hosting option avoids platform fees entirely
Used by established events like PyCon, MozFest, and PyConCascades
Supports both anonymous and open review workflows for talk selection
cons: Exact pricing for public/managed events is not published
Self-hosting requires technical setup and ongoing maintenance
No AI features mentioned
No mobile app is stated

faqs.question: Is Pretalx free?
faqs.answer: Yes, testing is free with no hidden costs; payment is only required to make a managed event public.

faqs.question: Is Pretalx open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, pretalx is open-source software with publicly available source code and community contributors.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Pretalx?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting is available alongside the managed hosting option at pretalx.com.

faqs.question: What review processes does Pretalx support?
faqs.answer: Both anonymous and open review processes for evaluating talk submissions.

faqs.question: Who uses Pretalx?
faqs.answer: Events including PyCon, MozFest, and PyConCascades are listed as users.

use_cases.title: Running a Call for Papers
use_cases.description: Organizers open customizable CFP forms, then review submissions anonymously or openly before building a schedule.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted conference management
use_cases.description: Technical teams self-host pretalx to run the full CFP-to-schedule workflow on their own infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pretalx?
paragraphs: Pretalx is open-source conference management software covering the full workflow from Call for Papers to a published schedule. Organizers can customize CFP forms, choose anonymous or open review of submissions, email speakers, and build multi-track schedules that update automatically when changed.

The software can be self-hosted or run on pretalx's managed hosting at pretalx.com, where testing is free and payment is only required once an event goes public. Source code is publicly available, with a plugin ecosystem for further customization.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pretalx is for
paragraphs: Pretalx is used by conference organizers of various sizes, including established events like PyCon, MozFest, and PyConCascades, as well as smaller community-run conferences that want an open-source, self-hostable alternative to proprietary CFP tools.

enrichment.short_description: Tito is a pay-per-ticket event registration platform with no subscription fees, built for developer and B2B conferences.
enrichment.starting_price: 3% per ticket (2.5% for approved charities/nonprofits); free for free events
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tito: pay-per-ticket event registration platform with API access, used by technology, developer, and B2B conferences.

features.title: Event dashboard
features.description: Manage registrations, attendees, and payments from a central dashboard.

features.title: Attendee event pages
features.description: Customizable pages for attendees to register and manage tickets.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Full API access for custom integrations.

features.title: Embeddable widget
features.description: Add ticket sales to any website with a two-line embed.

features.title: Custom CSS & JavaScript events
features.description: Style the checkout and hook into JavaScript events for custom behavior.

features.title: Built-in email
features.description: Send emails directly to attendees from the platform.

features.title: Self-service receipts
features.description: Attendees can access their own receipts without contacting support.

pros: No subscription fees or setup costs; pay only per ticket sold
Free events incur no platform fee
Discounted 2.5% fee for approved charity, nonprofit, and community events
Full developer API and embeddable widget for custom integrations
Reports processing over €600,000,000 in customer sales
cons: Standard 3% platform fee plus separate payment gateway fees (e.g., Stripe) apply on top
Charity/nonprofit discount requires an application and approval
Founding year and company size are not published
No AI features mentioned

faqs.question: How much does Tito cost?
faqs.answer: There's no subscription; Tito charges 3% per ticket on standard paid events (2.5% for approved charities/nonprofits), and nothing for free events.

faqs.question: Where is Tito based?
faqs.answer: Tito is headquartered at 27 Frederick Street South, Dublin, Ireland.

faqs.question: Does Tito have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tito offers developer API access alongside an embeddable ticket widget.

faqs.question: What payment processors does Tito support?
faqs.answer: Stripe and PayPal, supporting 139 currencies.

faqs.question: Is there a fee cap?
faqs.answer: Yes, on paid plans Tito caps its per-ticket fee at €25 for high-priced tickets.

use_cases.title: Developer & tech conference ticketing
use_cases.description: Technology and B2B conference organizers use Tito's dashboard and API to sell and manage tickets.

use_cases.title: Embedding ticket sales on an existing site
use_cases.description: Organizers embed Tito's widget directly into their own website with two lines of code.
use_cases.audience: Organizers with an existing event website

use_cases.title: Charity and community events
use_cases.description: Approved charity, nonprofit, and community events qualify for a reduced 2.5% ticket fee.

features: No platform fee on free tickets

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tito?
paragraphs: Tito is an event registration and ticketing platform for free and paid events. Organizers manage registrations, process payments, and communicate with attendees from a central dashboard, with a developer API and an embeddable ticket widget for adding sales to an existing website.

Rather than charging a subscription, Tito charges per ticket sold: 3% on standard paid events, 2.5% for approved charity, nonprofit, and community events, and nothing on free events. The company is headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, and reports processing more than €600,000,000 in ticket sales for its customers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tito is for
paragraphs: Tito is aimed at technology, developer, and B2B conference organizers who want registration tooling with an API and embeddable widget rather than a fixed monthly subscription.

enrichment.short_description: Ticket Tailor is a UK event ticketing platform charging a flat per-ticket fee instead of a percentage-based booking fee.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ticket Tailor: flat-fee event ticketing software with no booking-fee percentage, founded in 2010 and based in London.

features.title: Flat per-ticket fees
features.description: Charges a fixed fee per ticket instead of a percentage of the ticket price.

features.title: Pay-as-you-go or prepaid credits
features.description: Choose pay-as-you-sell pricing or buy discounted ticket credits upfront.

features.title: Direct payments
features.description: Payments go directly to the organizer's own Stripe, PayPal, or Square account.

features.title: First 5 tickets free
features.description: New organizers sell their first 5 tickets at no cost.

features.title: Discounts for good causes
features.description: Reduced fees for B Corps, PTAs, charities, and not-for-profits.

pros: No percentage-based booking fee; flat per-ticket pricing keeps costs predictable
First 5 tickets are free for every organizer
Certified B Corporation
Discounted fees available for charities, PTAs, and other good-cause organizers
Payments settle directly to the organizer's own payment processor account rather than being held by the platform
cons: Payment processing fees (Stripe/PayPal/Square) apply on top of Ticket Tailor's own fee
Lower per-ticket rates require buying credits in bulk upfront (minimum 100 credits)
No AI features mentioned
Company size not publicly disclosed

faqs.question: How does Ticket Tailor charge for tickets?
faqs.answer: A flat fee per ticket rather than a percentage of the ticket price — as low as £0.22/ticket with prepaid credits, or £0.60/ticket pay-as-you-go.

faqs.question: Are the first tickets free?
faqs.answer: Yes, organizers can sell their first 5 tickets at no cost.

faqs.question: Where is Ticket Tailor based?
faqs.answer: London, United Kingdom; the company was founded in 2010.

faqs.question: Does Ticket Tailor take a cut of my ticket revenue?
faqs.answer: No — payments go directly to the organizer's own Stripe, PayPal, or Square account.

faqs.question: Are there discounts for nonprofits?
faqs.answer: Yes, discounts are available for good causes, B Corps, PTAs, and not-for-profits.

use_cases.title: Small to mid-sized ticketed events
use_cases.description: Organizers who want predictable, flat per-ticket costs instead of a percentage cut of ticket revenue.

use_cases.title: Charity and community ticketing
use_cases.description: Charities, PTAs, and not-for-profits access reduced per-ticket fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ticket Tailor?
paragraphs: Ticket Tailor is an event ticketing platform, founded in 2010 and headquartered in London, that lets promoters and venues sell tickets online while keeping payments in their own Stripe, PayPal, or Square account rather than routing money through the platform.

Instead of a percentage-based booking fee, Ticket Tailor charges a flat fee per ticket — as low as £0.22 with prepaid credits, or £0.60 on a pay-as-you-sell basis — and every organizer's first 5 tickets are free.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ticket Tailor is for
paragraphs: Ticket Tailor is aimed at independent organizers, venues, charities, and community groups who want predictable, flat per-ticket pricing and direct control of their payment processing, rather than a percentage-based booking fee.

enrichment.short_description: Humanitix is a charity-backed event ticketing platform that directs 100% of its profits to charitable causes.
enrichment.starting_price: 2.1% + $0.99 per paid ticket (Standard); free for free events
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Humanitix: event ticketing platform where 100% of profits go to charity, with reduced fees for charities and schools.

features.title: Free events at no cost
features.description: Free events incur zero platform fees.

features.title: Standard ticket fee
features.description: 2.1% + $0.99 per paid ticket, plus payment processing of 2.9% + $0.30.

features.title: Charity & school discount
features.description: Reduced 1% + $0.99 fee for approved charities and schools.

features.title: Website integration
features.description: Sell tickets directly through an organizer's own website.

features.title: Reserved seating & gift cards
features.description: Support for reserved seating and gift card sales.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Connects with Mailchimp, Salesforce, Facebook, and Canva.

features.title: Event discovery marketplace
features.description: Public listing and discovery of events by category and location.

pros: 100% of profits go to charity; Humanitix operates as a registered charity itself
No subscription fees, contracts, or sign-up costs
Reduced fees for charities and schools (1% + $0.99 vs. standard 2.1% + $0.99)
Free events cost nothing to run
Built-in integrations with Mailchimp, Salesforce, Facebook, and Canva
cons: Payment processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) apply in addition to Humanitix's own ticket fee
Custom/enterprise pricing requires contacting sales
Founding year and headquarters are not published
No AI features mentioned

faqs.question: How does Humanitix make money for charity?
faqs.answer: 100% of Humanitix's profits are donated to charity; it operates as a registered charity itself.

faqs.question: What does Humanitix charge for paid tickets?
faqs.answer: 2.1% + $0.99 per ticket (Standard), plus 2.9% + $0.30 payment processing, or 1% + $0.99 for approved charities and schools.

faqs.question: Is Humanitix free for free events?
faqs.answer: Yes, free events incur no ticketing fees.

faqs.question: What integrations does Humanitix support?
faqs.answer: Mailchimp, Salesforce, Facebook, and Canva, among others.

faqs.question: Does Humanitix require a subscription?
faqs.answer: No — there are no subscription fees, contracts, or sign-up costs.

use_cases.title: Charity and school events
use_cases.description: Charities and schools access a reduced 1% + $0.99 per-ticket fee.

use_cases.title: Free community events
use_cases.description: Organizers running free events pay no ticketing fees.

use_cases.title: Festivals and conferences wanting a cause-driven platform
use_cases.description: Organizers who want ticket-fee profits to support charitable causes choose Humanitix over standard for-profit ticketing platforms.

features: Discounted rate for approved charities and schools

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Humanitix?
paragraphs: Humanitix is an event ticketing platform built around a charitable business model: it describes itself as directing 100% of its profits to charity and operates as a registered charity itself. Organizers use it to sell tickets to festivals, conferences, live shows, and other events.

Pricing is fee-based rather than subscription-based: free events cost nothing, standard paid events are charged 2.1% + $0.99 per ticket plus payment processing, and approved charities and schools receive a reduced 1% + $0.99 rate.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Humanitix is for
paragraphs: Humanitix is aimed at event hosts — including festivals, conferences, live shows, and charities — who want their ticketing fees to fund charitable giving, with additional discounted pricing specifically for charities and schools.

enrichment.short_description: Luma is a free-forever event page and ticketing platform with a paid Plus plan that removes platform fees.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Luma (lu.ma): free event page and ticketing platform with a paid Plus plan offering 0% fees, API access, and Zapier.

features.title: Event pages
features.description: Create a page for an event, invite guests, and sell tickets.

features.title: Unlimited free events
features.description: The Free plan supports unlimited events and guests.

features.title: Guest check-in
features.description: Track and check in attendees at the door.

features.title: Ticket types & coupons
features.description: Set up multiple ticket types, group purchases, and coupon codes.

features.title: Zoom integration
features.description: Connect events to Zoom on the Free plan.

features.title: Zapier & API access
features.description: Luma Plus adds Zapier automation and API access.

features.title: Custom event URLs & tax collection
features.description: Plus plan supports custom event page URLs and tax collection on ticket sales.

pros: Free plan supports unlimited events and guests at no cost
Free plan accepts major cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and regional payment methods
Luma Plus removes the 5% platform fee entirely (0% on paid events)
API access and Zapier automation available on the Plus plan
Multiple weekly-send add-on tiers let high-volume hosts scale their newsletter reach
cons: Free plan charges a 5% platform fee on paid ticket sales
Weekly invite/newsletter sends are capped (500 on Free, 5,000 on Plus, extra tiers cost more)
API access requires the paid Plus plan
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published

faqs.question: Is Luma free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports unlimited events and guests, with a 5% platform fee on paid events.

faqs.question: What does Luma Plus add?
faqs.answer: 0% platform fee on paid events, tax collection, custom event URLs, Zapier automation, API access, and higher weekly send limits.

faqs.question: How much does Luma Plus cost?
faqs.answer: $59/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Does Luma integrate with Zoom?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoom integration is available on the Free plan.

faqs.question: Can I increase my weekly email sending limit?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plus subscribers can purchase add-on packages raising the limit from 5,000 up to 100,000 weekly sends.

use_cases.title: Free community events
use_cases.description: Hosts use the Free plan to create event pages and manage RSVPs and check-in at no cost.

use_cases.title: Paid ticketed events without platform fees
use_cases.description: Organizers upgrade to Plus to avoid the 5% fee on paid ticket sales and access tax collection.

use_cases.title: Developers automating event workflows
use_cases.description: Plus subscribers use API access and Zapier integration to connect Luma to other tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Luma?
paragraphs: Luma (lu.ma) is an event platform for creating an event page, inviting guests, and selling tickets. Its Free plan supports unlimited events and guests, guest check-in, multiple ticket types, and payment acceptance through major cards, Apple Pay, and Google Pay, with a 5% fee applied to paid ticket sales.

A paid tier, Luma Plus, removes that platform fee entirely, and adds tax collection, custom event page URLs, Zapier automation, API access, and higher weekly invite-send limits, priced at $59/month billed annually.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Luma is for
paragraphs: Luma suits hosts running free or low-volume events on its no-cost Free plan, as well as recurring or higher-volume ticketed event organizers who upgrade to Plus to eliminate platform fees and connect Luma to other tools via its API.

enrichment.short_description: Partiful is a free event invitation app for planning parties with RSVP tracking, guest chat, and ticketing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Partiful: free party invitation app with customizable invites, RSVP tracking, guest polls, and ticketing.

features.title: Customizable invitations
features.description: Design invites with custom backgrounds, fonts, and animations.

features.title: Guest list & reactions
features.description: Guests can see the guest list and leave comments and reactions.

features.title: Text blast messaging
features.description: Send message updates to all guests at once.

features.title: Polling
features.description: Poll guests to pick a date or time for an event.

features.title: Payment collection & ticketing
features.description: Collect payments or sell tickets for an event.

features.title: QR code check-in
features.description: Check guests in at the door with QR codes.

features.title: Photo album sharing
features.description: Guests can share photos in a shared album after the event.

pros: Entirely free with no paywalls
Available on web, iOS, and Android
Highly rated: 5.0 stars across roughly 40,000 App Store ratings, 200,000+ ratings overall
Supports payment collection and ticket sales despite being free to use
Invite distribution via email, text, Instagram, WhatsApp, and phone contacts
cons: No stated business or enterprise tier for formal conference-scale event management
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published
No AI features mentioned

faqs.question: Is Partiful free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Partiful is free to use with no paywalls.

faqs.question: What platforms is Partiful available on?
faqs.answer: Web browser, iOS, and Android.

faqs.question: Can I collect payments through Partiful?
faqs.answer: Yes, Partiful supports payment collection and ticket sales for events.

faqs.question: How do guests check in?
faqs.answer: Hosts can use QR code check-in at the event.

faqs.question: How is Partiful rated?
faqs.answer: It has around 40,000 App Store ratings at 5.0 stars, and over 200,000 ratings overall.

use_cases.title: Casual social gatherings
use_cases.description: Hosts create customizable invites and track RSVPs for parties and casual get-togethers.
use_cases.audience: Social event planners, particularly Gen Z users

use_cases.title: Ticketed private events
use_cases.description: Hosts collect payments or sell tickets for events directly through Partiful invites.
use_cases.audience: Hosts charging for entry or contributions

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Partiful?
paragraphs: Partiful is a free event invitation platform for planning parties and social gatherings. Hosts create customizable invitations with custom backgrounds, fonts, and animations, then track RSVPs, poll guests on timing, and message the full guest list.
Beyond invitations, Partiful supports QR code check-in, payment collection and ticket sales, and post-event photo sharing, and is available on web, iOS, and Android.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Partiful is for
paragraphs: Partiful is aimed at people planning casual social events and parties — the platform notes particular popularity among Gen Z users — who want a free, highly customizable invitation and RSVP tool rather than a formal conference-management system.

enrichment.short_description: Zuddl is a unified B2B event management platform covering ticketing, virtual/hybrid production, onsite tools, and AI agents.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zuddl: unified event platform for B2B webinars, hybrid, and in-person events with AI agents, starting at $10,000/year.

features.title: Ticketing & registration
features.description: Landing page builder for event registration and ticketing.

features.title: Virtual & hybrid production
features.description: Video production and streaming tools for virtual and hybrid events.

features.title: Onsite tools
features.description: Check-in, badge printing, and lead capture for in-person events.

features.title: Mobile attendee app
features.description: Customizable app for attendee networking and engagement.

features.title: Speaker & exhibitor portals
features.description: Dedicated portals for managing speakers and exhibitors.

features.title: Zuddl AI Agents
features.description: AI-powered agents built to reduce manual event-operations work.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Direct integrations with sales and martech tools, described as native rather than Zapier-based.

features.title: Analytics & gamification
features.description: Event analytics, reporting, and gamification features.

pros: Single platform spans digital, field, and full conference event types
Includes AI-powered agents for event operations
Native integrations with sales and martech tools rather than relying solely on Zapier
Unlimited onsite staff included for check-in and lead capture
Volume-based pricing that becomes more efficient as attendee and organizer counts scale
cons: Minimum commitment starts at $10,000/year for 2 organizer licenses — not aimed at small events
Additional collaborators cost extra ($170/month annually)
Detailed feature-by-tier breakdown, founding year, and headquarters are not published
No self-serve or free tier

faqs.question: How much does Zuddl cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing starts at $10,000/year for a minimum of 2 organizer licenses, scaling with attendee volume.

faqs.question: Does Zuddl have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, "Zuddl AI Agents" are built into the platform to reduce manual event-operations work.

faqs.question: What event types does Zuddl support?
faqs.answer: Digital (webinars/virtual), field/in-person, and unified conference programs, depending on license tier.

faqs.question: Are onsite staff limited?
faqs.answer: No, unlimited onsite staff are included for check-in and lead capture.

faqs.question: Does Zuddl offer native integrations?
faqs.answer: Yes, the platform states it offers native integrations with sales and martech tools rather than relying on Zapier.

use_cases.title: B2B webinars and virtual events
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use the Digital License for registration, communications, and analytics on virtual programs.

use_cases.title: Hybrid field events
use_cases.description: Organizers combining in-person and digital programming use the Digital + Field License.

use_cases.title: Large unified conference programs
use_cases.description: Organizations running digital, field, and full conference programs together use the Unified License.

features: Full access across digital, field, and conference event types

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zuddl?
paragraphs: Zuddl is a unified event management platform for B2B companies, associations, agencies, and communities, covering ticketing and registration, virtual and hybrid event production, onsite check-in and badge printing, and speaker/exhibitor management from a single system.

The platform includes "Zuddl AI Agents," described as built to reduce manual dependencies and speed up event operations, plus native integrations with sales and martech tools. Licensing starts at $10,000/year for a minimum of 2 organizer licenses, with pricing scaling by attendee volume rather than per event.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zuddl is for
paragraphs: Zuddl targets B2B marketing and event teams, associations, agencies, and enterprise organizations running webinars, field events, and full conference programs who need one platform across digital and in-person formats rather than separate tools for each.

name: Welcome
skip_reason: Official site (experiencewelcome.com and www.experiencewelcome.com) returned HTTP 404 on the homepage, /pricing, and /about pages across multiple fetch attempts. Third-party sources indicate the company unexpectedly shut down its service, suggesting the product may be discontinued. Could not verify current facts from a reachable, operating official site.
